About Me

The Person
Behind the Code.

I'm a Software Engineering graduate from York University with hands-on experience across the full stack, from designing relational databases and RESTful APIs to building responsive front-end interfaces with React.

I care deeply about writing clean, maintainable code and solving real problems efficiently. Whether it's optimizing a SQL query, architecting a system, or shipping a polished UI, I bring curiosity and precision to everything I build.

Beyond coding, I've developed strong communication and teamwork skills through diverse work environments from logistics operations to retail, giving me a well-rounded perspective when collaborating across teams.

Personal Projects

Things I've Built.

01

CineCircle — Movie & TV Review Platform

Full-stack MERN web app inspired by IMDb, Letterboxd, and Reddit. Users can register, browse media, write reviews with category ratings (story, acting, cinematography), comment, upvote/downvote, and see real-time viewer counts via WebSocket. Built with a three-tier layered architecture, JWT authentication, bcrypt password hashing, server-side input validation, and a full Jest + Supertest test suite covering 28+ test cases. Deployed live on Vercel (frontend) and Render (backend) with MongoDB Atlas.

React Node.js MongoDB TypeScript Express WebSocket JWT Jest
Live Demo → GitHub →
02

York University Online Parking System

A fully-featured Java GUI parking management application for students and staff at York University. Supports client registration with unique IDs, booking creation/editing/cancellation, real-time space tracking, multi-method payment processing (debit, credit, mobile), and a dedicated Manager Interface for adding and viewing managers. Architected using 5 design patterns — Factory, Singleton, Observer, Chain of Responsibility, and Strategy — with a component-based UML design and MVC separation between controllers and models.

Java Java Swing MVC Design Patterns UML OOP
GitHub →
03

TAB2XML — Music Tablature to Score Converter

Java application that converts music tablature into MusicXML and renders it as a visual music score — making notation accessible for both novice and expert musicians. Supports guitar and drum tablature, with features including live music sheet preview, note playback (guitar), PDF export, measure navigation, and side-by-side tablature/XML/sheet views. Built with Gradle and Eclipse, following structured requirements and testing documentation.

Java MusicXML Gradle Eclipse IDE PDF Export Audio Playback
GitHub →
04

Auction E-Commerce Platform

Full-stack e-commerce auction platform supporting both Forward Auctions (highest bid wins) and Dutch Auctions (first "Buy Now" wins instantly). Built with a Spring Boot microservices backend and Flask-rendered frontend, using a layered MVC + Pub-Sub architecture. Features include JWT + bcrypt OAuth2 authentication, real-time bid processing with concurrency handling, payment processing with shipping options, receipt generation, item cataloguing, and seller listing management. Includes 12 Postman-tested API endpoints, SQL injection resistance, load testing via JMeter, and a 0% final failure rate across all test cases.

Spring Boot Java Flask SQLite JWT OAuth2 Microservices JMeter
Frontend → Backend →
05

Automated Plant Watering System

Full IoT system built with Arduino Uno and MATLAB that autonomously monitors soil moisture and temperature in real time. When soil voltage drops below 1V (dry threshold), the system activates a water pump, flashes a red LED, and triggers a buzzer alert. A green LED confirms wet soil and stops the pump. Features a live-updating dual graph (moisture vs. time, temperature vs. time), a manual stop button, and data logging every 10 seconds — designed to make plant care easier, especially for seniors.

Arduino Uno MATLAB IoT Grove Sensors PWM Live Data
Watch Demo →
06

York University Pinball Machine — ENG 4000 Capstone

Restored a 1974 'Bon Voyage' arcade machine for York University's ENG 4000 Capstone as part of a 6-person interdisciplinary team. As Software Engineer, I built the full game software in Python — featuring a coin-detection state machine (Insert Coin → Running → Game Over), real-time score tracking via Arduino analog pins (A0 = 10pts, A1 = 100pts, A2 = 1000pts), rotating MP4 video backgrounds rendered with OpenCV and Pygame, outlined text rendering for live score and countdown timer, a persistent top-10 high score leaderboard, and full audio management including background music, coin start sound, and game over cues. Hardware communication was handled via pyfirmata with a custom iterator for non-blocking analog pin reads.

Python Pygame Arduino OpenCV pyfirmata State Machine Embedded Systems Real-Time Systems
